diagrams.title: Database System Overview
diagrams.description: A visual representation showing the purpose of a database system within an organization, illustrating how users and applications interact with the database through DBMS to store, retrieve, and manage data efficiently.
diagrams.purpose: Explains the fundamental purpose of a database as an organized collection of data and how the DBMS acts as an intermediary between users and the physical data storage.

diagrams.title: Key Characteristics of Database Systems
diagrams.description: A visual diagram displaying the seven essential characteristics of database systems: Data Abstraction, Data Independence, Data Integrity, Data Security, Concurrent Access, Data Recovery, and Efficient Query Processing, each represented as interconnected components.
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates the core characteristics that differentiate database systems from traditional file processing, helping learners understand why databases are essential for modern data management.

diagrams.title: Traditional File Processing vs Database Approach
diagrams.description: A comparison diagram showing two sides: traditional file processing (with data redundancy, inconsistency, security issues, and isolation problems) versus the database approach (with data integration, consistency, security, and centralized control).
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates the advantages of using a database system over traditional file processing methods, highlighting the purpose of databases in solving common data management problems.
